trivia_bullets.text: Knight of the Last Breath is typed as a Giant Knight — an unusual creature type pairing. Giants in Ravnica lore are typically associated with the Gruul rather than the Orzhov, making this figure a guild-contracted outlier: a Giant pressed into Orzhov service as an enforcer [Scryfall].

The card carries both Afterlife 3 and an activated sacrifice ability — two distinct ways to convert creatures into 1/1 flying Spirit tokens. The Wizards RNA mechanics article frames Afterlife as the Orzhov's expression of "supernatural staying power": even death is just another form of service [magic.wizards.com/en/news/feature/ravnica-allegiance-mechanics]. EDHREC rank 22,199 puts it in seldom-played territory for Commander, consistent with its role as a Limited uncommon.

No flavor text on this card [Scryfall]. The absence is notable: Orzhov Syndicate cards in RNA frequently carry flavor text reinforcing the guild's extraction ideology. This one lets the rules text speak alone — sacrifice another creature, gain spirits, die and gain more spirits. The mechanic is the message.

Artist Milivoj Ceran (RNA-188) depicts the knight in sealed-helm plate with no visible face — a design choice consistent across several Orzhov enforcer cards where anonymity reinforces the institutional-over-individual register of the guild. The Orzhov enforcers do not have names; they have functions [Scryfall].
